page: Amazon Web Services r7a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor operating at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory tasks. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench ranks in the top 10%, supported by high-speed DDR5 memory read bandwidth. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, which contribute to top-tier scores in Passmark encryption and physics tests. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the strong and top-tier categories respectively, while LLM inference benchmarks show strong to top-tier processing speeds for small, medium, and large models.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated compute resources, the r7a.24xlarge offers high resource density for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The absence of local storage requires external block storage integration, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider. This instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-bound data processing pipelines, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory read bandwidth, and strong database operation capabilities. This profile makes the r7a.24xlarge highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including in-memory databases, large-scale data processing, software compilation, and LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise databases and data processing.
